new_classes.label_en: Distillation process from grape wine or grape marc
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process that uses grape wine or grape marc as the base material, including primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine (wine distillate / raw distillate). The resulting product does not have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol or a spirit drink in the narrow sense.

new_classes.label_en: Grape wine or grape marc base quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a distilled spirit indicating that its distillation base is grape wine or grape marc (pomace), distinguishing CN 2208 20 products from spirits derived from other raw materials.

new_classes.label_en: Spirits obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (CN 2208 20)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol with an alcoholic strength of less than 80 % vol; spirits obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (including wine distillate / raw distillate of wine), classified under CN subheading 2208 20. The product is produced by a distillation process whose base material is grape wine or grape marc and it bears the quality of a grape wine or grape marc base. It does not have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol nor of a spirit drink in the strict sense (if it is a raw distillate).

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three primary discriminating criteria found in the legal text: (1) the product is a spirit/distillate obtained by distillation (linked via SpiritousDistillationProcess subclass), (2) the base material is grape wine or grape marc (GrapeWineMarcBaseQuality), and (3) it falls within the < 80% vol undenatured ethyl alcohol heading (UndenaturedEthylAlcoholLessThan80VolQuality). The legal note also states the product includes "wine distillate" (raw distillate) and explicitly notes it does NOT have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol nor a spirit drink in the strict sense. This nuanced negative condition (complement of neutral ethyl alcohol / spirit drink character) cannot be fully captured without additional sibling classes being defined for neutral ethyl alcohol and "proper" spirit drinks within 2208. No such sibling classes are yet in the TBox, so this exclusion condition is omitted, reducing coverage slightly. All positive sufficient conditions are present.
